threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a ‘London bus’ instead of just ‘the bus’. Everyone here knows it’s a local lifeline, not just public transport.
Last night, a bloke on the 59 was singing 'Wonderwall' so loudly, the whole bus joined in. Classic Brixton madness at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing two kids argue over who gets the last piece of döner on a Tuesday afternoon, right by the canal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how Kreuzberg's street art always seems to reflect the latest protest? It’s like the walls whisper what the city feels.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they still call it 'the Elbo Room' instead of that 'new' name. Tourists don’t know the real dive bars.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a group of tourists trying to take a selfie with a broken Muni sign—totally oblivious to the bus zooming past them. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'festa major' is just a party, but it's a whole neighborhood vibe—decoration, traditions, community spirit. It's personal.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still try to pay with cash at the bakery, unaware that most places prefer card now.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' - this is Dalston, mate, we don’t do subterranean.
Last night on the 133, a guy was passionately explaining why he believes Brixton's buskers should be licensed. Didn’t realise it was such a big debate!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I walked by a toddler sharing a donut with a stray cat by the canal. That mix of chaos and kindness encapsulates Neukölln perfectly.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they have a 'silent disco' in public parks where everyone dances with headphones on. Feels surreal under the trees!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name all the taco trucks on 16th without Googling. The hidden gems speak more than the tourist spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a pigeon taking a ride on the back of a Muni bus today. Only in SF do even the birds know how to travel smart.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all Gaudí—don't forget the hidden plazas where locals gather, especially after 9pm. That's where the real vibe is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to find a nearby supermarket without resorting to Google Maps, while locals know the small shops off the beaten path.
